---
title: "nitrix.stats.priors"
generated: true
signature-hash: 0606f7bc556deec9
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.stats.priors`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

## `PriorFn` {#PriorFn}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:PriorFn -->
```python
PriorFn = Callable[[Float[Array, '']], Float[Array, '']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:PriorFn -->
*TODO: describe this type alias / constant.*
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `halfnormal_prior` {#halfnormal_prior}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:halfnormal_prior -->
```python
halfnormal_prior(sd: float) -> PriorFn
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:halfnormal_prior -->
Half-normal prior on the lengthscale `rho` (penalises large `rho`).

`p(rho) ~ exp(-rho**2 / (2 sd**2))` on `rho > 0`, so
`-log p(rho) = rho**2 / (2 sd**2)` (up to a constant): a soft cap that pulls
the estimate **down**, away from over-smooth (near-linear) fits.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:halfnormal_prior -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `sd` | `float` | — | — | The half-normal scale. Smaller `sd` is a stronger pull toward small `rho`; a sensible value is on the order of the covariate range. |

**Returns** `PriorFn`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `invgamma_prior` {#invgamma_prior}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:invgamma_prior -->
```python
invgamma_prior(a: float, b: float) -> PriorFn
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:invgamma_prior -->
Inverse-gamma prior on the lengthscale `rho` (penalises small `rho`).

`p(rho) ~ rho**(-a-1) exp(-b / rho)` on `rho > 0`, so
`-log p(rho) = (a + 1) log rho + b / rho` (up to a constant).  The `b/rho`
term diverges as `rho -> 0`, so this pulls the estimate **up**, away from a
lengthscale that collapses into noise-fitting -- the usual
penalised-complexity-style regulariser for a weakly-identified `rho` (e.g.
sparse or short series).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:invgamma_prior -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `a` | `float` | — | — | Shape and scale (`a, b > 0`). The prior mode is `b / (a + 1)`; pick `a, b` so that is a plausibly-small lengthscale. |
| `b` | `float` | — | — | Shape and scale (`a, b > 0`). The prior mode is `b / (a + 1)`; pick `a, b` so that is a plausibly-small lengthscale. |

**Returns** `PriorFn`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `lognormal_prior` {#lognormal_prior}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:lognormal_prior -->
```python
lognormal_prior(mu: float, sd: float) -> PriorFn
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:lognormal_prior -->
Log-normal prior on the lengthscale `rho` (median `exp(mu)`).

`p(rho) ~ (1/rho) exp(-(log rho - mu)**2 / (2 sd**2))` on `rho > 0`, so
`-log p(rho) = (log rho - mu)**2 / (2 sd**2) + log rho` (up to a constant):
a symmetric-in-`log rho` pull toward the prior median `exp(mu)` with
multiplicative spread `sd`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:lognormal_prior -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `mu` | `float` | — | — | Prior mean of `log rho` (so `exp(mu)` is the prior median lengthscale). |
| `sd` | `float` | — | — | Prior standard deviation of `log rho` (`> 0`). |

**Returns** `PriorFn`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
